string.text: The MP-40, also known as the Maschinenpistole 40, stands as an iconic World War II-era submachine gun that gained widespread recognition for its distinctive appearance and reliable performance. Developed by Nazi Germany during the late 1930s, this compact and lightweight firearm became the standard issue submachine gun for German infantry during the war. Featuring a distinctive folding stock, a relatively moderate rate of fire, and chambered for the 9mm Parabellum cartridge, the MP-40 was prized for its accuracy and ease of use in close-quarters combat. Its innovative design and menacing appearance have since made it an enduring symbol of the era, often depicted in various forms of media as a representation of military technology during World War II. \n \n
Standard magazine for the MP40 Submachinegun, it holds 30 rounds of 9x19mm ammunition. \n \n
